summary: The SNOT-22 is commonly employed in specific clinical contexts like evaluating treatment responses and preoperative assessments in CRS but lacks strong support for being broadly or routinely applied across all patient care settings. Evidence does not confirm its wide-scale adoption despite recognition of its usefulness in tracking symptom-related health-related quality of life improvements.
